name: Trinification (SU(3)^3 GUT)
summary: Embeds the SM gauge group in SU(3)_C x SU(3)_L x SU(3)_R with a Z_3 symmetry permuting the three SU(3) factors. Each generation fits into the (3, 3, 1) + (3-bar, 1, 3) + (1, 3, 3-bar) representation (27 of E6 broken). Produces gauge coupling unification at the GUT scale, predicts vector-like leptons and a richer Higgs sector, and naturally suppresses proton decay by additional global symmetries.

addresses_puzzles.note: Three SU(3) factors unify at one scale via the Z_3 cyclic symmetry; with intermediate-scale matter, gauge couplings can converge cleanly. Mostly compatible with present alpha_s precision data with intermediate-scale thresholds.
27 contains an SU(2)-singlet neutrino per generation enabling type-I seesaw with M_R at intermediate scale.
SU(3)_C x SU(3)_L x SU(3)_R unifies fermions into 27 of E_6; constrains lepton-quark Yukawa relations (de Rujula-Georgi-Glashow 1984).

touches_benchmarks.note: Trinification's gauge structure suppresses dimension-6 proton-decay operators; predicted lifetimes typically 10^36-10^38 yr, well above Super-K bounds.

new_fields: additional matter beyond SM 16-plet (full 27 of E6 per family)
new_symmetries: SU(3)_C x SU(3)_L x SU(3)_R x Z_3
introduces.note: Three gauge couplings (Z_3-equal at GUT scale), Higgs and Yukawa structure, intermediate-scale physics.

history: de Rujula, Georgi, Glashow (1984), 'Trinification of all elementary particle forces', presented at the Fifth Workshop on Grand Unification (Brown University), in K. Kang, H. Fried, P. Frampton (eds.), 'Fifth Workshop on Grand Unification', World Scientific, Singapore (1984). Active modern variants connect to E6 GUT and B-anomaly model-building.

references.citation: de Rujula, Georgi, Glashow, 'Trinification of all elementary particle forces', in 'Fifth Workshop on Grand Unification' (eds. Kang, Fried, Frampton), World Scientific, Singapore (1984)
